FREE GUIDE
6 Anti-Dandruff
DIY Recipes
Neem, clay, black seed oil… the homemade treatments that actually calm the scalp — ingredients, prep, application, frequency.
by Schicgirl
Type 4 coily hair · 4A 4B 4C · © 2024–2026 Schicgirl
Start here

Not all dandruff is the same

"Anti-dandruff" isn't one single category. Dandruff has 3 different causes — and each recipe in this guide works best for one of them.
💧 Dry scalp

Small, fine white flakes, tight/itchy scalp. Often worse in winter or after a stripping shampoo.

🍂 Buildup

Bigger flakes, scalp feels oily. Too much unrinsed product, or excess sebum.

🦠 Yeast (Malassezia)

Persistent flakes despite washing, sometimes itchy. The most common cause of dandruff that keeps coming back.

Which recipe for which cause

Dry scalp → Black seed oil (recipe 3), Pure aloe vera mask (recipe 5).

Buildup → Rhassoul clay mask (recipe 6), Apple cider vinegar rinse (recipe 2).

Yeast / persistent → Neem mask (recipe 1), Diluted tea tree (recipe 4), Apple cider vinegar rinse (recipe 2).

When to see a doctor

These recipes help manage and space out common dandruff. If your scalp is red, very irritated, crusty, or nothing improves after several weeks of consistent care, see a dermatologist — it could be seborrheic dermatitis or something else that needs targeted treatment.

Your DIY recipes

Recipes 1 to 3

1 · Neem maskAntifungal
For: persistent dandruff linked to yeast (Malassezia).
Ingrédients
  • 2 tbsp neem powder
  • Warm water (for a smooth paste)
  • 1 tsp jojoba or coconut oil
Préparation

Mix the neem powder with warm water little by little until you get a smooth, lump-free paste. Add the oil and mix again.

Application

Apply to dry or slightly damp scalp, section by section, using your fingertips (not nails).

Fréquence1x/week, then every 2 weeks once improved · leave on 20-30 min under a cap
2 · Apple cider vinegar rinseRebalancing
For: product buildup, oily-feeling scalp, yeast.
Ingrédients
  • 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar (unfiltered)
  • 1 cup (250 ml) water
Préparation

Dilute the vinegar in the water. Pour into a bottle or cup for application.

Application

After shampooing, pour over the scalp, massage 2-3 min with fingertips. Rinse with clean water or let it air-dry (the smell fades as it dries).

Fréquence1-2x/week max · do not use on irritated or broken skin
3 · Black seed oil (nigella)Soothing
For: dry, tight scalp that flakes in fine patches.
Ingrédients
  • 1-2 tbsp pure black seed oil
  • Optional: 1 tbsp coconut oil if scalp is sensitive
Préparation

Warm the oil for a few seconds between your palms. Mix with coconut oil if using.

Application

Massage into the scalp with fingertips, small circles, section by section.

Fréquence2x/week · leave on 30 min to overnight under a bonnet
SCHICGIRL — Anti-Dandruff Recipes  ·  schicgirl.me  ·  3
Your DIY recipes (continued)

Recipes 4 to 6

4 · Diluted tea treeAntifungal
For: persistent dandruff, yeast — the most scientifically studied option.
Ingrédients
  • 3-5 drops tea tree essential oil
  • 2 tbsp carrier oil (jojoba or olive)
Préparation

ALWAYS dilute the essential oil in the carrier oil — never apply pure to skin. Mix well.

Application

Massage into the scalp with fingertips, section by section.

Fréquence1-2x/week · leave on 15-20 min · patch test on your inner elbow 24h before first use
5 · Pure aloe vera maskSoothing
For: itchiness, dry or irritated scalp — the gentlest recipe, safe to use often.
Ingrédients
  • 3-4 tbsp pure aloe vera gel (99%+)
Préparation

Nothing to prepare if using ready-made gel — otherwise extract the pulp from a fresh leaf.

Application

Apply directly to the scalp, massage gently.

Fréquence2-3x/week · leave on 20 min · a small amount can be left in without rinsing
6 · Rhassoul clay maskAbsorbing
For: buildup, excess sebum, dandruff that sticks to the scalp.
Ingrédients
  • 3 tbsp rhassoul (or green clay)
  • Warm water
  • 1 tsp honey (optional, softens)
Préparation

Mix the clay with warm water in a non-metal bowl (use a wooden or plastic spoon) until smooth.

Application

Apply to the scalp and roots.

Fréquence1x/week · leave on 10-15 min max — never let the clay dry out completely, it over-dries the scalp
SCHICGIRL — Anti-Dandruff Recipes  ·  schicgirl.me  ·  4
The recap

Do & Don't

Do
  • Identify your cause before picking a recipe
  • Try 1 recipe for 3-4 weeks before judging
  • Patch test 24h for any essential oil
  • Massage in small circles, never scratch
  • Space out treatments if scalp gets too dry
Don't
  • Pure essential oil on skin
  • Let clay dry out completely
  • Scratch the scalp (worsens irritation)
  • Stack several recipes the same day
  • Ignore a red/crusty scalp that persists
The honest promise

These recipes calm and space out common dandruff for many women — but they don't replace medical advice if your scalp stays red, crusty, or very irritated after several weeks of consistent care.
